─[ Ceph VLAN ARP Conflict ]─ ┐

name: ceph-vlan-arp-conflict description: Diagnose and fix ARP poisoning when LXC containers share IPs with Proxmox hosts on the Ceph VLAN user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Bash

Ceph VLAN ARP Conflict

Context

Proxmox LXC containers with a veth on vmbr1 (Ceph bridge) can silently conflict with the host’s IP on the same bridge. Two devices respond to ARP for the same IP, causing intermittent Ceph failures that look like network issues.

Snags

  • Ceph Mons rotating in/out of quorum (different Mon each time)
  • OSD heartbeat failures: no reply from 10.0.210.x osd.N since back/front
  • Slow heartbeats (5-20+ seconds)
  • VMs fail to start with “mount error” (RBD unavailable)
  • Ceph briefly reaches HEALTH_OK then degrades again
  • ip neigh show dev vmbr1 shows different MACs for the same IP on different nodes

Diagnosis

# Check ARP tables on all nodes — look for mismatched MACs
# Replace <node-ip> with IPs from docs/knowledge/environment/
ssh root@<node-ip> 'ip neigh show dev <ceph-bridge>'

# Compare host bridge MAC with what neighbors see
ssh root@<node-ip> 'ip link show <ceph-bridge>'

# Check container IPs on the Ceph VLAN
ssh root@<node-ip> 'pct exec <ctid> -- ip addr show eth0'

If node A sees IP X with MAC-1 but node B sees IP X with MAC-2, there’s a conflict.

Workaround

Runtime Fix

# Remove conflicting IP from the container
ssh root@<node-ip> 'pct exec <ctid> -- ip addr del <conflicting-ip>/24 dev eth0'

# Flush ARP on ALL nodes
ssh root@<node-ip> 'ip neigh flush dev <ceph-bridge>'

# Restart affected Ceph services
ssh root@<node-ip> 'systemctl restart ceph-osd@<id> && systemctl restart ceph-mon@<name>'

Permanent Fix

Update the container’s network config in the host so the conflicting IP is not assigned on boot. Containers should use dedicated IPs from the allocated range (see docs/knowledge/environment/inventory/).

Prevention

Use non-overlapping IP ranges on the Ceph network (see docs/knowledge/environment/ for allocation strategy):

  • Hosts: [e.g., lower range]
  • Containers: [e.g., upper range]
  • Never assign a host IP to a container interface on the same bridge.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
